title: FCoP Failure & Recovery
description: Abstraction 5 of 7 — Failure. Per ADR-0019 (which addresses the most pointed external-architecture critique: 'A real runtime is not the happy path; it is how anomalies survive'). v1.0 freezes 4 failure modes + 5 recovery actions + a minimal Session-recovery hook. POSIX analogy: this is FCoP's errno + signal-handler. Each Failure MUST be paired with at least one Recovery (the pairing rule is enforced at runtime, not in schema, since the mapping is implementation-defined per ADR-0019 §decision).

failureType.description: Per ADR-0019 §failure-modes. FROZEN at 4 for v1.0. TIMEOUT = agent missed `timeout_at`; CRASH = host adapter / reference impl exited abnormally; DEADLOCK = mutual wait between agents; DRIFT = agent output does not match the TASK contract.

recoveryAction.description: Per ADR-0019 §recovery-actions. FROZEN at 5 for v1.0. RETRY = same agent redoes same TASK; RESUME = load session state and continue; ROLLBACK = git-revert to pre-TASK state; ABORT = terminate TASK with status=aborted; ESCALATE = lift to next governance layer.

failureRecord.title: Failure record
failureRecord.description: Per ADR-0019 §design-details — `Project.report_failure(failure)` accepts this shape and emits a FAILURE_DETECTED event (event.schema.json).

evidence.description: Free-form evidence. Per ADR-0019, suggested fields: `last_seen_at`, `exit_code`, `stderr_tail`, `expected`, `actual`, `cycle` (for DEADLOCK).

recoveryRecord.title: Recovery record
recoveryRecord.description: Per ADR-0019 §design-details — paired with a FailureRecord; emitted as RECOVERY_INITIATED then RECOVERY_COMPLETED events.

outcome.description: Recovery outcome. `succeeded` triggers RECOVERY_COMPLETED with this value; `failed` triggers RECOVERY_COMPLETED with this value; `in_progress` means RECOVERY_COMPLETED has not yet fired.

trigger_failure_id.description: ID of the FailureRecord that triggered this recovery (correlation key).

sessionRecoveryCall.title: Session recovery call
sessionRecoveryCall.description: Per ADR-0019 §session-recovery-hook. The call contract for `Project.recover_session(session_id, action)`. v1.0 minimal surface; full Session schema (LLM context, tool history) is deferred to v1.x.

session_id.description: Per ADR-0019 — implementation-defined; reference impl uses '<task_id>:<agent_code>'.

action.description: Per ADR-0019 — only 3 of the 5 recovery actions are exposed via this hook (RETRY and ESCALATE are caller-side decisions, not session-state operations).

metadata.description: Free-form bag for v1.x Session schema migration; v1.0 reference impl ignores.
